methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Oklo
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Nano Nuclear Energy, a public-stage nuclear energy company, trades within a defined peer universe of 11 comparable companies across the advanced reactor sector—3 publicly traded (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 in late-stage or Series D private funding. The company has raised $600M+ to date and currently trades at a $1.5B+ valuation.

The peer-median methodology applies directly to this valuation exercise. A cohort of 4 same-sector comparables—TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Kairos Power ($1.6B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B)—yields a median valuation of $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.0B. This peer set reflects mixed stages (late-stage private and IPO) within the nuclear energy infrastructure space, providing a reasonable anchor for relative valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The current $1.5B+ market valuation aligns with the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include the timing of regulatory milestones for advanced reactor deployment, technology-specific risk across modular versus other reactor designs within the peer set, and the capital intensity required to move from development to commercial operation. The single-method result should be supplemented with additional cost-of-capital or discounted cash flow analysis as underlying technical risks resolve.
